title: potentilla apennina subsp. kionaea
intro: Potentilla apennina subsp. kionaea, also known as the Kionaea cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla apennina subsp. kionaea include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried herb, tea, or tincture, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
